rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/24/2023. The following procedural issues must be cleared two weeks prior to hearing. 1. Petition to include what relief being sought. 2. Notice of Hearing to be given to all parties who are entitled to. PC 12201(b). 3. Proposed Order must be submitted prior to the hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. 4. Information re: if there is a change in finances Payment of the court filing fees as Petition alleges the estate is solvent and the applicant has not reported any change in his/her finances.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/17/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 05/15/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Notice must be given to the former attorneys of record for the petitioner. CRC 7.704. If the former attorneys agreed to waive statutory compensation, a signed waiver must be filed. 4) Even if statutory compensation is not sought, the amount of compensation payable must be stated with its calculation. CRC 7.705. 5) All the information required by CRC 7.403 must be provided as to each creditor claim. 6) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250? 7) Was the decedent's LLC interest (listed on the inventory and appraisal partial no. 2) his separate property, community property, or both? 8) What is the amount of the estate cash on hand representing the decedent's separate property? 9) What is the amount of the estate cash on hand representing the decedent's one-half share of community property? 10) If the petitioner is the sole trustee of the devisee trust established by the decedent and petitioner, all beneficiaries of the trust entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b) must be identified. 11) Please provide a copy of a court order that authorized the stipulated termination of the decedent's separate property trust. If no court authorization has been obtained, please address the trustee's authority to terminate the trust. 12) If the decedent's separate property trust has been terminated, does Article 3 of the will require a testamentary trust to be established on the same terms and conditions? 13) The information required by CRC 7.550(b)(2) and (3) must be provided as to each non-cash asset inventoried and appraised (but not part of the assets on hand), even if an account has been waived. 14) Is the estate solvent? CRC 7.550(b)(5).

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE ANITA SECKINGER 2017 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/10/2023 (no earlier date available) to be reviewed and adjudicated with the related conservatorship fee petition filed in PCN-15-299267. 1) Paragraph 8 of the petition: i) No hourly fee schedule or statement of compensation has been filed re the petitioner's new rate ($200 per hour). Probate Code sections 2614.8 & 2643.1(a)*. ii) Did the 07/13/2021 order issued in the related conservatorship case authorize the petitioner to pay any fees on account for his services as trustee, in addition to his services as conservator? 2) Paragraph 10: The estimated annual income appears to be understated based on the most recent social security and annuity payments ($1,354.90 + $136.44). 3) Schedule A: Did the petitioner obtain prior court approval to liquidate the Jackson National Life annuity (policy number ending in 9892) in August 2021? Probate Code section 2459(c)*. *See LRSF 14.47(B)(5). 4) Exhibit B: The attorney's services on 03/15/2023 & 04/12/2023 were not billed at the new rate ($495 per hour). 5) The telephone number for a Zoom appearance stated on the remote appearance information attached to the proof of service of notice must be updated.

(3) It does not appear that a (redacted) copy of the statement verifying the guardianship estate's share of funds in "Guardian's Savings Account" has been filed. Why is the balance the same on 9/30/21 as on 10/1/19? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/13/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2) Request For Admissions; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Petitioner and Respondent Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. sections 2030.300(d), 2031.310(h), 2031.320(b), 2033.290(d). The court does not find that Respondent Monica Zimmerman acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel further responses or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Based on all of the evidence before the Court, Monica Zimmerman is ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions on this motion in the amount of twenty-two thousand dollars ($22,000), within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2) Request For Admissions; And For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] Per the Notice of Motion, Rudolph seeks an order compelling Respondent Monica Zimmerman ("Monica") to produce documents and for further responses to (1) Rudolph Koppl's Request for Production of Documents to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("RPD") Nos. 41 through 53 served, on January 25, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2031.310 and 2031.320, and (2) Rudolph Koppl's Request for Admissions to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("RFA") Nos. 15, 21, 22, 23, 30 and 31, served on January 25, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2033.290, on the grounds that the requests seek information, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter of the action and the objections thereto are without merit or too general. The motion also seeks an award of monetary sanctions in the amount of $17,500 pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. sections 2023.030, 2031.310 and 2033.290. Monica's responses to the Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admissions at issue in this motion are entirely in the form of various objections, with no substantive responses. Any party to an action may obtain discovery regarding any matter not privileged that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, unless otherwise limited by court order in accordance with the Discovery Act. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010). Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery, or of any other party to the action. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010.) Rudolph has filed "Amended Petition to Interpret Trust." The Zimmerman's have filed "Petition to Determine Existence of Nonexistence Of Power, Duty, Or Right; To Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass Or Be Delivered; And To Pass Upon The Acts Of Frida Koppl, As Then Trustee." Both petitions are filed in connection with the Frida Koppl Trust One dated August 5, 2011 (the "Trust") and are pending before the court. Facially, the discovery requests at issue in this motion seek matter, either itself admissible in evidence or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending trust action. California's discovery statutes "must be construed liberally in favor of disclosure unless the request is clearly improper by virtue of well-established causes for denial." (Greyhound Corp. v Superior Court (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355, 377.) Any doubts "should generally be resolved in favor of permitting discovery." (Williams v Superior Court (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 542 quoting Pacific Tel. & Tel. Co. v Superior Court (1970) 2 Cal.3d 161, 173.) Based on the record before the Court, Request for Production of Documents No. 43 has not been propounded previously in discovery proceedings in this action. Based on the record before the Court, Request for Admissions No. 15, 21, 22, 23, 30, and 31 has not been propounded previously in discovery proceedings in this action. Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Compel Further Responses to (1) Request for Production of Documents and (2) Request for Admissions; and for Sanctions is GRANTED. Respondent Monica Zimmerman is ordered to produce documents and further responses to (1) Rudolph Koppl's Request for Production of Documents to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("RPD") Nos. 41 through 53 served, on January 25, 2023, and (2) Rudolph Koppl's Request for Admissions to Monica Zimmerman, Set Two ("RFA") Nos. 15, 21, 22, 23, 30 and 31, served on January 25, 2023. Further responses shall be served no later than June 30, 2023. [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2) Request For Admissions; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Compel Further Responses to (1) Request for Production of Documents and (2) Request for Admissions; and for Sanctions. Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman lodge objections to and seek to strike as inadmissible certain portions of the moving papers, termed as numbered "specification" by Respondents. The court rules on the objections as follows: SPECIFICATION No. 1 Rudolph's Separate Statement, 8:20-22.] OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 2, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 10:12-14; 11:18-20; 20:9-11. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 3, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 9:4-5. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 4, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 9:22-24; 11:3-4; 12:7-8; 13:4-6; 14:2-3; 14:28-15:2;.16:4-5; 16:27-17:1; 17:25-27; 18:23-25; 19:22-24; 20:27-28. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 5, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 9:25-26. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 6, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 9:27-10:2. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 7, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 33:20. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 8, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 33:21-24. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 9, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 41:14-16. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 10, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 47:12-15. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 11, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 47:16-18. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 12, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 49:25-50:1. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 13, Rudolph's MPA, 3:14-16. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED SPECIFICATION No. 14, Rudolph's MPA, 3:17-19. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 15, Rudolph's MPA, 4:3-7. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 16, Rudolph's MPA, 5:21-26. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 17, Rudolph's MPA, 7:9-12. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 18, Rudolph's MPA, 7:21-26. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 19, Rudolph's MPA, 7:9-12. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 20, Rudolph's MPA, 7:25-8:1. OBJECTION: Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 21, Zabronsky Declaration, 7:18-19. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAR WAI LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (3) There was no Exhibit A or Exhibit B attached to the petition. (4) Paragraph 7 of the petition sets forth the incorrect appraised value of the estate. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be checked when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) (5) It is unclear why another $435 filing fee was paid for this petition, when it appears that the petition filed on 7/5/22 had been continued numerous times and should just have been amended. (6) The last sentence of paragraph 20 of the petition refers to repaying herself funeral expenses? This is also included in the prayer, as is an unsupported request for $6,000 for attorney fees for extraordinary services. (Was the decedent male or female? Isn't the administrator male (a brother)?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANG WU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/23. Petition to specifically allege in detail that how the decedent's 1/3 interest in the real property should be distributed. The petition proposed the 1/3 interest to be distributed to the decedent's two children, bypassing the distribution to the estate of the decedent's post-deceased's spouse under PC 13100, without elaborating said estate's entitlement (shares). Petition alleges the decedent's estate was separate property. Distribution needs to be proposed under PC 6401(c)(3). Petition needs to elaborate how and what the estate of the post-deceased spouse is entitled to and why probate proceeding is not necessary for her estate. (1) Petitioner filed 13100 affidavit on the estate of Koon Hai Wu; however, the affidavit appears inappropriate for the reason listed above. (2) Personal representative of the estate of estate of Koon Hai Wu seems necessary for the reason listed above. (3) Notice of Hearing with notice of directions for remote appearance to be given to Wieland Wu. These procedural issues must be addressed two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/23 pursuant to Petitioners' status report and email exchange with Counsel. Additionally, the following issues with the accounting petition must be addressed in time to deliver courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 9/1/23. (1) The only correct form of Notice in Probate matters is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the face of which (item 1) the FULL AND CORRECT TITLE OF THE PETITION must be set forth, and on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a COPY OF THE NOTICE OF HEARING THAT WAS SERVED MUST BE ATTACHED to it. (Please do NOT file pleading forms of proofs of service; they are really not appropriate in Probate.) (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Notice of Remote Appearance is not required in Probate. Giving notice to all interested parties of how to appear remotely, however, IS critical. Please review the local rules and the court's website. (3) Original FINANCIAL INSTITUTION statements are required to be filed to verify the (cash) assets on hand. [LRSF 14.34 D.] (Not all of the two "Declarations" filed on 5/24/23 and received on 5/31/23 have been reviewed in detail yet.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHLEEN MARIE SANCHEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication has been filed. (2) The petition caption is incomplete (no reference to IAEA authority). (3) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (4) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/27/23. (A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address the information missing from his petition.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It is unclear why a waiver of accounting and waiver of notice of proposed action were filed when the petitioner is the proposed personal representative and reportedly the sole heir)
